rent.note: furnished 1BR in nomad beach neighborhoods (Lagoa, Ingleses, Barra); R$4,200–7,000/mo (~$750–$1,250)

utilities.note: electric + water + internet; beach areas can spike electric in summer

groceries.note: home cooking; Brazilian supermarkets well-stocked; BRL favorable to USD

diningOut.note: mix of prato feito (cheap) and beach restaurants (mid-range)

transit.note: buses + occasional rideshare; car/moto helpful in beach suburbs

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly; strong coworking growth driven by nomad boom

misc.note: gym, toiletries, surf lessons, beach lifestyle, entertainment, buffer

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base. S. hemisphere summer (Dec-Feb) peaks for beach destinations.

evidence.claim: comfortable nomad budget ~$1,150–$1,500/month; day-to-day beyond rent $520–$620
evidence.quote: A comfortable month for one person runs roughly US$1,150; day-to-day living beyond rent runs just US$520 to US$620 a month, putting a comfortable all-in nomad budget around US$1,250 to US$2,000
furnished apartments and houses in the popular neighbourhoods rent for 3,500 to 7,000 reais ($700 to $1,400); average rents near R$60/sq meter — making a 70 sqm apartment roughly R$4,200/month

evidence.claim: nomad boom driving growth and rising prices
evidence.quote: Florianópolis: a Nomad Boom and Its Rising Bill
a comfortable solo lifestyle there runs R$9,000–12,000/month (~$1,670–$2,220), comfortably within the digital nomad visa income threshold
